why: The SoR (core.builds.sources._local_path) and the Console (isCanonicalFileUri in
web/src/pages/Import.tsx) must accept EXACTLY the same set of source uris. When they
drift, a source registered via API/CLI is buildable from one side and permanently
unrunnable from the other — the Console marks it unresolvable and blocks every build
for the project. Two of the six Codex findings on PR #71 were that drift (a malformed
escape, which Python's unquote leaves literal but decodeURIComponent throws on; and a
single-slash file:/x, which urlparse accepts and the Console's ^file:/// refuses).
This corpus is read by BOTH suites — tests/test_builds_sources.py (pytest) and
web/src/pages/fileUriGate.test.ts (vitest) — so parity is mechanically enforced rather
than asserted in a comment. A pure differential cannot catch a defect the two gates
SHARE (file:///C: read the worker's cwd on both), so each side also keeps its own
display==read oracle; this fixture is the parity half.

reject.why: query stripped from the read path
fragment stripped from the read path
NUL names no file on any filesystem
dot segments resolve away from the display
pipe IS the Windows drive separator
colon outside the drive position re-roots
encoded drive separator (structure is read pre-decode)
bare drive is DRIVE-RELATIVE (the worker's cwd on C)
single-slash: reads the same, splits enforcement
malformed escape: literal to unquote, throws in the Console
hex-valid but invalid UTF-8: unquote yields U+FFFD (mangled read), the Console throws

accept.why: the drive ROOT resolves absolute (unlike a bare C:)
a directory really named 100%, canonically spelled
a VALID multi-byte UTF-8 escape — the dual of the invalid-UTF-8 reject; non-ASCII filenames must stay registerable
an uppercase scheme is accepted by both gates (uri.lower() / the /i flag) — pinned so neither drops it
a literal ';' survives ONLY because 'file' is absent from urllib.parse.uses_params — load-bearing and non-obvious, so pinned
